question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that peers.ToRosetta() is called on the result of addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) without checking if the returned AddressBookEntries pointer is nil, and if the repository returns a nil pointer with a nil error, cause a nil pointer dereference panic that crashes the service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send a sustained stream of /network/status requests that each cause the latestNodeServiceEndpoints SQL query to scan the full address_book_entry table, causing table-level read contention that blocks write operations (address book updates) from completing in a timely manner, degrading the mirror node's ability to update peer information?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that errors.Errors is a package-level global slice that is populated at init time and then returned by reference in every NetworkOptions() response, and by flooding /network/options, cause the same slice to be serialized thousands of times per second, creating unnecessary serialization CPU load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any per-IP or per-connection rate limiting on /network/status to perform a low-and-slow attack — sending requests just below any global rate limit threshold — that continuously consumes DB connections and prevents the connection pool from ever being fully available to legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the context passed to NetworkStatus() is propagated to all three DB calls, and by sending requests with a context that carries a large deadline far in the future, cause DB queries to run for an extended period without timeout, holding DB connections open and starving legitimate requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Impacts caused by griefing with no economic damage to any user on the network'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /network/status endpoint performs no input validation on the NetworkRequest body before executing DB queries, allowing the attacker to send requests with arbitrary metadata fields that are silently ignored but still parsed, consuming CPU and memory for deserialization on every request?
